steps.title: Find the absolute increase in the bill
steps.content:
Subtract the original bill from the new bill to find the increase in dollars:
$$79.86 - 75.74 = 4.12$$
The bill increased by 4.12 dollars before converting that change to a percent.

steps.title: Calculate the percent increase and round
steps.content:
Use the original bill as the base for percent increase:
$$\frac{4.12}{75.74} \times 100\% \approx 5.44\%$$
Rounding to the nearest tenth of a percent gives 5.4%. The original bill is the denominator because percent increase is measured relative to the starting value. That is the correct answer.
